frameworks.name: suppressive/inhibitory processing (GABAergic)
frameworks.one_line: Learning sharpens perceptual decisions by GABA-mediated suppression in cortex.

frameworks.name: perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: Training-induced improvement in perceptual judgments through noise filtering.

frameworks.name: functional brain connectivity
frameworks.one_line: GABA measures combined with connectivity to dissociate local vs inter-cortical mechanisms.

core_question: Do suppressive (GABAergic) processes in decision-related and visual areas support learning-dependent improvements in perceptual judgments?
key_assumptions: Learning improves perception by filtering external noise and task-irrelevant information
GABA concentration indexes local suppressive/inhibitory processing
Different perceptual tasks recruit distinct suppression mechanisms
manipulations: Task type: target detection vs feature discrimination
Brain region: parietal vs visual cortex
key_findings: Parietal GABA relates to suppression of task-irrelevant information
Learning-dependent changes in visual GABA relate to enhanced target detection and feature discrimination
Target detection training involves local connectivity and disinhibition of visual cortex
Feature discrimination training involves inter-cortical interactions linked to suppressive visual processing
interpretations: Suppressive interactions in decision-related and visual areas facilitate perceptual judgments during training
Distinct suppressive mechanisms (local disinhibition vs inter-cortical suppression) support different perceptual learning tasks
